Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Descriptive copula-less equative clause; adjective phrase modifying noun with implicit existential meaning.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A quiet person may possess deep thoughts, intelligence, or hidden strength.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: An tawo nga diri yakanon bangin madalom maghunahuna.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family advice, education, politics
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Maritime metaphor rooted in coastal life in Baybay where calm waters often conceal depth.
First Example Sentence: Ayaw pagminos ha iya kay an tubig nga hilom, madalom.
Second Example Sentence: Bisan waray hiya imik, hinumdumi nga an tubig nga hilom, madalom.
Third Example Sentence: Ha politika, an tubig nga hilom, madalom gud.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative existential clause with relative modifier.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Not everything that looks attractive or valuable truly is.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Diri tanan nga maopay an hitsura may tinuod nga bili.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Adapted from Iberian proverb traditions during colonial contact.
First Example Sentence: Pag-amping ha mga investment kay diri tanan nga nagkikinang bulawan.
Second Example Sentence: Natutdo an amon maestro nga diri tanan nga nagkikinang bulawan.
Third Example Sentence: Ha gugma, diri tanan nga nagkikinang bulawan.

Idiom (Cham): Naglalakat nga murag may kalayo ha tiil.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Progressive verb phrase with simile introduced by 'murag'.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Moving in great haste as if pressured or anxious.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rural imagery referencing walking barefoot on hot ground.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Cham): /naɡ.laˈla.kat ŋa ˈmu.raɡ maj kaˈla.jo ha tiˈil/
First Example Sentence: Kay ano ka naglalakat nga murag may kalayo ha tiil?
Second Example Sentence: Nagawas hiya nga murag may kalayo ha tiil han nakadungog han balita.
Third Example Sentence: An bata naglalakat nga murag may kalayo ha tiil kay nahadlok.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative marker 'baga hin' plus negative existential noun phrase.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nagbubuhat hiya hin diri maaramon nga paagi.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Common household expression in Visayan-speaking regions.
First Example Sentence: Ayaw pag-inom hin sobra, baga hin waray buot ka na.
Second Example Sentence: Nagdesisyon hiya dayon nga baga hin waray buot.
Third Example Sentence: Diri ako magbubuhat hin sugad, diri ako baga hin waray buot.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Fire metaphor symbolizing inner emotion in Austronesian oral tradition.
First Example Sentence: May kalayo ha dughan hiya han ginbinuangan.
Second Example Sentence: Naklaro nga may kalayo ha dughan an lider han nagyakan.
Third Example Sentence: Bisan nahiyom, may kalayo ha dughan.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Reflexive causative verb with locative complement.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ginbabalewaray niya an tinuod nga kamatuoran.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in civic discourse during community assemblies.
First Example Sentence: Diri kita dapat magpapabulag ha kamatuoran.
Second Example Sentence: An opisyal gin-akusaran nga nagpapabulag ha kamatuoran.
Third Example Sentence: Kun nagpapabulag ka ha kamatuoran, magruruba an komunidad.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Life has unpredictable ups and downs.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: An kinabuhi may magkalain-lain nga kahimtangan.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Coastal livelihood metaphor central to Baybay identity.
First Example Sentence: An kinabuhi sugad hin dagat, usahay malinaw usahay mabaskog.
Second Example Sentence: Ginhingadlan niya an libro nga An Kinabuhi Sugad hin Dagat.
Third Example Sentence: Para ha iya, an kinabuhi sugad hin dagat nga waray kasigurohan.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Progressive verb with object phrase metaphor.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Metal imagery influenced by blacksmithing traditions.
First Example Sentence: An paghukom hini nagkikinahanglan hin puthaw nga kasingkasing.
Second Example Sentence: Nagkikinahanglan hin puthaw nga kasingkasing an pagdumara han krisis.
Third Example Sentence: Basi magmalampuson, nagkikinahanglan hin puthaw nga kasingkasing.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Passive perfective verb phrase with locative complement.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Placed in a difficult or awkward situation.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nabutang hiya ha makuri nga kahimtangan.
First Example Sentence: Nabutang ha alanganin an empleyado tungod han sayop.
Second Example Sentence: Diri ko karuyag mabutang ha alanganin.
Third Example Sentence: An pamilya nabutang ha alanganin han nagkasakit an amay.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Progressive verb with object phrase and locative metaphor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Leaves a deep emotional or psychological impact.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Nakaapekto gud ito hin marig-on ha iya pagbati.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in oral storytelling traditions emphasizing spiritual interiority.
First Example Sentence: An gera nagbibilin hin marka ha kalag han komunidad.
Second Example Sentence: An iya mga pulong nagbibilin hin marka ha kalag.
Third Example Sentence: May mga eksperyensya nga nagbibilin hin marka ha kalag tubtob kamatayon.
